summary: type-slint fetches and eval's remote code from a third-party IPFS gateway on require
details: type-slint impersonates the pino logger (exports a `pino` alias, mimics pino's file layout, declares logger-related keywords) while its main middleware's only real action is to spawn lib/caller.js as a detached child process. lib/caller.js issues an HTTPS GET to a hardcoded Pinata IPFS gateway URL (https://bronze-improved-gibbon-411.mypinata.cloud/ipfs/bafkreiav2siin2dxemm5w5w533rd42jreawdoftldxg4zbjuoj2ob4a5a4) and passes the response body to `new Function.constructor('require', s)(require)`, executing the fetched JavaScript with full access to Node's require. lib/const.js and lib/caller.js also contain base64-encoded fallback URLs decoding to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/XRGF3 and https://jsonkeeper.com/b/4NAKK, labeled as `DEV_API_KEY` to disguise them. Requiring the package as a logger triggers the detached fetch-and-eval of attacker-controlled code on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
